findings.message: Client adds 'include_location' query parameter, but committed consumer pact does not expect it. The pact test defines .with_request('GET', '/inventory/1') without parameters, creating a contract mismatch when the mock server runs.
findings.suggestion: Regenerate the consumer pact by running the inventory contract test (test_consumer_inventory.py) so the committed pact reflects the new parameter expectation, OR remove the parameter addition if the provider has not been updated to support it.

findings.message: Client accesses 'location' field from response, but committed consumer pact only defines {'item_id': int, 'in_stock': int}. This field is not part of the contracted response.
findings.suggestion: Update the consumer pact test to include 'location' in the expected response body, then regenerate the pact file. Alternatively, remove the location access if the provider does not supply it.

findings.message: Consumer pact appears to not have been regenerated after the client change. The inventory pact file (pacts/demo-inventory.json) is missing or not published after the client was modified.
findings.suggestion: Run the consumer pact test to regenerate and publish the updated pact to pacts/ directory and ensure it is committed before this client change is deployed.
